column2: RCF/TSCF pH dependence of weak acids (ion trap)
column4: RCF & TSCF up to 100x higher at pH 4 than pH 7
column11: Mechanism: only the NEUTRAL fraction permeates well. PFAS are ~fully anionic (f_d=~1) at paddy pH -> minimal neutral route -> LOW passive TSCF baseline; observed translocation must be channel/carrier-driven (rows above).

column8: Generic ionizable PPCP uptake model, 2023 (ET&C 42:793); Schriever & Lamshoeft 2020
column11: Template for f_xy(pH, species). CAUTION: Henderson-Hasselbalch/logD alone is insufficient at f_d=~1 - pair with your GHK electrodiffusion.

column4: sigmoidal (Dettenmaier, logKow -0.8 to 5, max at low end); bell-shaped (Briggs)
column8: Dettenmaier, Doucette & Bugbee, 2009; Briggs et al. 1982
column11: NON-IONIC reference only - upper bound where a neutral fraction exists; NOT directly valid for permanent anions. Doucette 2018 review compiles ionizable+non-ionic TSCF.

column4: shoot->root phloem transport demonstrated
column11: Confirms PFAS are PHLOEM-MOBILE -> justifies a phloem flux term. Magnitude/grain flux not quantified.

column4: grain solutes loaded EXCLUSIVELY via phloem (OsLCT1; OsHMA2 to panicle)
column11: ANALOG: rice grain is a phloem-fed sink (low grain transpiration). A PFAS-specific phloem->grain transporter is UNIDENTIFIED (gap). Favors short-chain/branched (more phloem-mobile).

column4: TF decreases with chain length; branched > linear isomers (more hydrophilic)
column11: Parameterize f_xy and phloem partition as DECREASING functions of chain length; add a branched/linear factor if you resolve isomers.

column4: long-chain (C>=7 PFCA, C>=6 PFSA) retained at root; short-chain bypass to vasculature
column8: Sources & transport in soil-plant systems, 2025 (New Contaminants)
column11: Mechanistic basis for a chain-length cutoff in f_xy / root retention (couples with your B_1 root binding).

column4: TF positively correlated with aboveground:root protein ratio
column11: Bridges to C4: protein-rich sinks (grain) retain PFAS -> B_grain via protein binding. Use to link f_xy and B_k.
